squat.camera_setup: Stand facing the camera or at a slight angle, 2-3 metres away. Full body from head to feet must be visible. A front or side angle both work well.
instructions: Stand with feet shoulder-width apart, toes slightly turned out
Keep your chest up and core braced throughout the movement
Push your hips back and bend your knees to descend
Lower until your thighs are at least parallel to the floor
Drive through your heels to return to standing
common_mistakes: Knees caving inward (valgus)
Excessive forward lean of the torso
Not reaching parallel depth
Rising onto toes instead of driving through heels
Rounding the lower back

deadlift.camera_setup: Side view is best for deadlifts --camera at hip height, 2-3 metres away. Full body including hands and feet must be visible to assess back angle.
instructions: Stand with feet hip-width apart, bar over mid-foot
Hinge at the hips and grip the bar just outside your knees
Keep your back straight and chest up
Drive through your legs and hips to lift the bar
Stand tall at the top, then reverse the motion to lower
common_mistakes: Rounding the lower back during the lift
Bar drifting away from the body
Jerking the weight off the floor
Hyperextending at the top
Hips shooting up before the chest

pullup.camera_setup: Position camera facing you from the front or at a slight angle. Full body from hands on the bar to feet must be visible. Step the camera back far enough to capture full extension.
instructions: Hang from the bar with an overhand grip, slightly wider than shoulders
Engage your lats and pull your chest toward the bar
Pull until your chin clears the bar
Lower yourself with control to full arm extension
Avoid swinging or kipping
common_mistakes: Using excessive kipping or swinging
Not achieving full range of motion
Chin not clearing the bar
Not fully extending arms at the bottom
Shrugging shoulders instead of engaging lats

pushup.camera_setup: Place your camera so it can see your FULL BODY from head to feet. Best position: to the side at floor level, 2-3 metres away. A front or diagonal angle also works --just make sure shoulders, elbows, hips and ankles are all visible.
instructions: Start in a plank position with hands slightly wider than shoulders
Keep your body in a straight line from head to heels
Lower your chest toward the floor by bending your elbows
Go down until elbows are at 90 degrees or chest nearly touches floor
Push back up to the starting position
common_mistakes: Hips sagging toward the floor
Hips piking up too high
Elbows flaring out to 90 degrees
Not going through full range of motion
Head dropping or looking up

plank.camera_setup: Side view is essential for planks --camera at floor level, 2-3 metres away. Your full body from head to feet must be in a straight line in the frame.
instructions: Start on forearms and toes, elbows directly under shoulders
Bend elbows to about 90 degrees - forearm plank, not high plank on hands
Keep your body in a straight line from head to heels
Keep head neutral - look at the floor just ahead of your hands
Engage your core and hold the position, breathing steadily
common_mistakes: Hips sagging toward the floor
Hips piking up too high
Doing a high plank on hands instead of forearms
Resting head on the floor
Head dropping or looking up
Elbows too far forward or backward

bicep_curl.camera_setup: Stand sideways to the camera with your working arm facing the camera. Full arm from shoulder to hand must be visible. Step back 2-3m so your full upper body is in frame.
instructions: Stand sideways to the camera, feet hip-width apart
Hold dumbbells with palms facing away from the camera
Keep your upper arms stationary against your torso
Curl the weights up by bending your elbows
Lower with control to full arm extension
common_mistakes: Swinging the body to generate momentum
Moving the upper arms/elbows forward
Not using full range of motion
Going too fast without control
Leaning back to lift heavier weight

tricep_dip.camera_setup: Side view only - camera 2-3 metres to your side at waist height. Full body from hands on bench to feet must be visible.
instructions: Sit at the edge of a bench, hands gripping the edge beside your hips
Extend legs forward with knees straight - body forms an L-shape
Press up to full arm extension to start
Lower by bending elbows to about 90 degrees
Press back up to full extension - keep torso upright
common_mistakes: Going too deep and stressing the shoulders
Bending knees instead of keeping legs extended
Leaning forward instead of staying upright
Not achieving full lockout at the top
Hips drifting too far from the bench

crunch.camera_setup: Side view at floor level, 2-3 metres away. Full body from head to feet must be visible. Ensure enough space to see legs and arms fully extended.
instructions: Lie on your back with legs extended and arms reaching forward
Lift legs and torso simultaneously, keeping arms extended
Meet in the middle forming a V-shape at the top
Lower back down with control -- feet never touch the floor
Keep arms straight throughout, reaching toward your shins at the top
common_mistakes: Letting feet touch the ground between reps
Bending the arms instead of keeping them extended
Using momentum instead of controlled movement
Not closing the hip angle enough at the top
Asymmetric movement -- one side leading

lateral_raise.camera_setup: Front view, camera at waist height, 2-3 metres away. Full upper body including both arms and hips must be visible.
instructions: Stand with feet shoulder-width apart, dumbbells at your sides
Keep a slight bend in your elbows throughout the movement
Raise arms out to the sides until level with your shoulders
Lead with your elbows, not your wrists
Lower the dumbbells back to your sides with control
common_mistakes: Bending elbows too much to shorten the lever arm
Flicking wrists above elbows at the top
Shrugging shoulders up toward ears
Swinging the torso for momentum
Raising arms above shoulder height (traps take over)
Uneven arm height -- one side higher than the other

side_plank.camera_setup: Side view facing the front of your body, camera at floor level, 2-3 metres away. Full body from head to feet must be visible.
instructions: Lie on your side supported by one forearm, elbow directly under shoulder
Stack your feet or stagger them slightly for balance
Lift your hips to form a straight line from head to feet
Rest your top arm on your hip or extend it upward
Hold the position, breathing steadily
common_mistakes: Hips sagging toward the floor
Hips piking up too high
Elbow not directly under the shoulder
Rotating the body toward the floor
Dropping the head out of alignment
Bending the knees (modified version)
